steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is D, $ f(x) = 31(6,561)^{\frac{x}{2}} $.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: The feature to display is $c$, the factor by which $f(x)$ multiplies for every increase of 2 in $x$. Compute $c$: from any form, $c = f(x+2)/f(x)$. Using the displayed form as the reference, $c = 3^{4 \cdot 2} = 3^8 = 6{,}561$.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: Rewrite the growth factor so an increase of $2$ in $x$ corresponds to one full power of the base: $3^{4x}=\left(3^8\right)^{x/2}=6561^{x/2}$.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content: Scan each choice for 6,561 appearing literally as the base: A has base 3, B has base 9, and C has base 81. Only D writes the base as $6{,}561$ with exponent $x/2$, so a step of 2 in $x$ raises the base to the first power exactly once.
